competition: FIFA World Cup 2026 · Group C · MetLife Stadium

items.title: Vini 'vanished' from Virginia's Instagram right before kickoff
items.text: Hours before the opener, eagle-eyed fans noticed Vini Jr had disappeared from Virginia's Instagram — and the web melted. Then he goes and scores the equaliser. Petty? Iconic? Both. The breakup arc is writing itself. 👻

items.title: Igor Thiago and the miss that broke Brazilian hearts
items.text: Vini whipped in a gorgeous cross and Igor Thiago somehow headed it to row Z. The timeline went full 'PELO AMOR DE DEUS.' We love him, but that one's going straight in the blooper reel. 🙈

items.title: Meanwhile outside: 'Honk if you love geese'
items.text: On the road to the MetLife, a dozen protesters turned up with handmade signs — 'Honk if you love geese' and 'No to gassing geese.' World Cup chaos has no offseason. Fans drove past absolutely baffled. 🪿

items.title: VINIIIII! Equaliser of pure spite — 1-1!
items.text: Bruno Guimarães fed him on the left, Vini left El Aynaoui for dead, cut inside and rifled it past Bounou. GAME ON. And remember — his ex Virginia is on the Globo broadcast watching him do it. The man scores when it matters AND when it stings. 💛💚

items.title: The whole timeline is screaming: VEM, ENDRICK!
items.text: Brazil went a goal down and the internet made up its mind — get the kid off the bench. Even with Vini levelling it, every Brazilian group chat wants Endrick on to go win it outright. Carlo, are you reading this? 👀

items.title: Ancelotti's pre-match quote is aging like milk
items.text: "The World Cup is won by whoever concedes the least, not whoever scores the most," said Carlo... and then Brazil shipped one on 19'. Vini's bailed him out for now — but the pundits already had the clip cued up. Relax, the man's won everything. Probably. 😅

items.title: WAG drama: Raphinha accused of being 'cold' on camera
items.text: The doc 'Convocadas' (about the Seleção's wives & girlfriends) has Brazil hooked. Fans piled on Raphinha for seeming cold to wife Natália Belloni — she fired straight back: "I'm treated like a princess." Mid-World Cup soap, exactly as ordered. 🍿

items.title: Morocco's secret weapon: Ancelotti knows them too well
items.text: Half of Morocco's engine room is Serie A-flavoured (Saibari, El Aynaoui) — ironic against an Italian-coached Brazil. Surprise call: El Khannouss got the left flank ahead of Hakimi as winger, with Achraf bombing on from full-back. Crafty. Annoyingly crafty. 😤

items.title: Morocco grab an early one — relax, it's nothing
items.text: Saibari poked one in on 19' and Morocco went in front. We said breathe — the Seleção have 90 minutes and the film had barely started. Vini hadn't even warmed up properly. Told you it'd get turned around. 💛💚

items.title: Virginia is covering the Cup... a week after the Vini split
items.text: Talked round by Luciano Huck and Angélica, Virginia Fonseca kept her Domingão slot and is in New York covering World Cup behind-the-scenes — right at her ex's opener. Him on the pitch, her on the broadcast. It's a soap opera inside the Cup. 👀

items.title: Times Square turned into a sea of green and yellow
items.text: Hours before kickoff the torcida turned the heart of New York into a Seleção cauldron — samba drums, flags and war chants between the skyscrapers. Nobody throws a party like Brazil. 🇧🇷

items.title: Why is the crowd whistling at the MetLife?
items.text: That loud hiss filling the stadium is the Moroccan support whistling every Brazil touch to put them off. Charming folklore — but the only noise that mattered was the roar when Vini's equaliser dropped. 😏
